senses.definition: a device or substance that causes rain or other forms of precipitation to occur or fall, often used in weather modification or laboratory contexts
examples: The smoke from the factory acted as a precipitating agent, forming clouds that could lead to rain.
Researchers tested a device claimed to be an atmospheric precipitating agent.

senses.definition: plural of precipitator; devices or substances that cause precipitation
examples: The lab used several precipitators to study cloud formation.
Industrial precipitators help reduce airborne particles.

senses.definition: the quality of being precipitous; the condition of something steep or sudden
examples: The precipititatousness of the cliff made the climb dangerous.
Her precipititatousness in decision-making worried her teammates.

senses.definition: an antibody that reacts with an antigen to form a precipitate in a laboratory test
examples: The assay used a sheep anti-precipitin antibody.
Precipitin tests help identify specific pathogens.

senses.definition: an antigen that stimulates the formation of precipitin antibodies
examples: The precipitinogen was introduced to elicit an antibody response.
Researchers studied the immune reaction to various precipitinogens.

senses.definition: capable of stimulating the production of precipitin antibodies
examples: Some proteins are precipitinogenic and trigger strong immune responses.
The precipitinogenic properties were essential to the assay design.

senses.definition: plural of precipitinogen; antigens that stimulate precipitin antibody formation
examples: Various precipitinogens were tested in the study.
The panel included several known precipitinogens.

senses.definition: antibodies that react with an antigen to form a visible precipitate
examples: The sample contained several precipitins.
Precipitin reactions are used to identify antigens.

senses.definition: very steep or sudden; happening very quickly or without enough consideration
examples: A precipitous cliff rose above the harbor.
The economy faced a precipitous decline after the scandal.
Her fall was precipitous, catching everyone by surprise.

senses.definition: in a way that is very steep or very sudden; happening quickly and without warning
examples: The stock price fell precipitously after the announcement.
The road drops precipitously here, so drive carefully.

senses.definition: the quality of being very steep or very sudden
examples: The precipitousness of the slope made the hike risky.
There was a sense of precipitousness in the market moves.

senses.definition: plural of precipitousness, the qualities of being precipitous
examples: The map showed several areas of precipitousnesses along the coast.
Unexpected precipitousnesses in the cliffside reduced the path's safety.

senses.definition: short for precipitates or precipitation; informally refers to sudden rain or precipitation events
examples: The forecast shows heavy precips this afternoon.
We carried an umbrella because of possible precips.

senses.definition: to circulate again or to move in a circular path before reaching final destination
examples: The newsletter was precirculated to the committee before the meeting.
Items were precirculated to ensure everyone saw the proposals.

senses.definition: having been circulated again or in advance of a meeting
examples: The proposal was precirculated to attendees.
Staff precirculated the briefing pack yesterday.

senses.definition: circulating again or in advance
examples: The report is precirculating for early feedback.
They are precirculating the agenda for the town hall.

senses.definition: the act of circulating again or in advance; advance distribution
examples: The precirculation of the report helped gather early comments.
Precircultion of materials is common in committees.

senses.definition: a short, clear summary of a book, speech, or article
examples: She wrote a concise precis of the chapter.
The lecturer provided a precis of the main ideas.

senses.definition: exact and accurate; clearly defined
examples: Please give me the precise time of arrival.
Her measurements must be precise for the experiment.

senses.definition: past tense of precise; to make precise or define exactly
examples: The contract was precised during negotiations.
They precised the requirements to avoid confusion.

senses.definition: exactly; in a strict and exact way
examples: That is precisely what I needed.
The plan is precisely laid out.

senses.definition: the quality of being exact or accurate
examples: Her preciseness in data entry reduced errors.
We value preciseness over speed in this analysis.

senses.definition: plural of preciseness; qualities of being precise
examples: The precisenesses of the measurements varied between trials.
We evaluated the precisenesses of different methods.

senses.definition: a person or thing that makes something more precise; a tool for refining measurements
examples: She acted as the preciser, tightening the tolerances.
The preciser improved the data accuracy.

senses.definition: third person singular present of precise; makes something exact
examples: The editor precises the manuscript before publication.
She precises the data to remove ambiguity.
